explanations.1: This line tries to load a .env file if it exists (the minus sign means 'don't fail if it's missing'), which probably contains your environment variables like database passwords.
explanations.4: The $$ is how you escape a dollar sign in makefiles—it becomes a single $ that the shell sees; here it's getting your user ID and group ID to pass to Docker so file ownership stays correct.
explanations.13: The minus sign at the start tells make 'keep going even if this command fails', useful here because the directories might not exist yet.
explanations.17: The \033 sequences are ANSI color codes that make the text yellow in your terminal; ${WEB_PORT} is a variable placeholder that gets replaced with your actual port number.
explanations.43: This target depends on 'reset', meaning 'reset' will run first before this target's commands execute.
